lede: A look at November 12 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Leonardo Puglisi and Russell Westbrook, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Boeing B-17 Flying Fortress
events.desc: A Boeing B-17 Flying Fortress and a Bell P-63 Kingcobra collide in mid-air over Dallas Executive Airport during an airshow, killing six.

events.title: Bid Kaneh explosion
events.desc: A blast in Iran's Shahid Modarres missile base leads to the death of 17 of the Revolutionary Guards members, including Hassan Tehrani Moghaddam, a key figure in Iran's missile program.

events.title: American Airlines Flight 587
events.desc: In New York City, American Airlines Flight 587, an Airbus A300 en route to the Dominican Republic, crashes minutes after takeoff from John F. Kennedy International Airport, killing all 260 on board and five on the ground.

events.title: Saudia
events.desc: A Saudi Arabian Airlines Boeing 747 and a Kazakh Ilyushin Il-76 cargo plane collide in mid-air near New Delhi, killing 349 in the deadliest mid-air collision to date.

events.title: Santa Cruz massacre
events.desc: Santa Cruz massacre: The Indonesian Army open fire on a crowd of student protesters in Dili, East Timor.

events.title: Oregon Department of Transportation
events.desc: The Oregon Highway Division attempts to destroy a rotting beached sperm whale with explosives, leading to the now infamous "exploding whale" incident.

events.title: 1970 Bhola cyclone
events.desc: The 1970 Bhola cyclone makes landfall on the coast of East Pakistan, becoming the deadliest tropical cyclone in history.

events.title: Suez Crisis
events.desc: In the midst of the Suez Crisis, Palestinian refugees are shot dead in Rafah by Israel Defense Force soldiers following the invasion of the Gaza Strip.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Battle of Gabon ends as Free French Forces take Libreville, Gabon, and all of French Equatorial Africa from Vichy French forces.

events.title: Pudge Heffelfinger
events.desc: Pudge Heffelfinger becomes the first professional American football player on record, participating in his first paid game for the Allegheny Athletic Association.

births.name: John William Strutt, 3rd Baron Rayleigh
births.line: English physicist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate (died 1919)

deaths.text: Steve Rexe, Canadian ice hockey player and coach
F. Holland Day, American photographer and publisher

observances.text: Birth of Sun Yat-Sen, also Doctors' Day and Cultural Renaissance Day. (Republic of China)
Christian feast day: Astrik (or Anastasius) of Pannonhalma
Christian feast day: Josaphat Kuntsevych (Roman Catholic Church, Greek Catholic Church)
